hero.title:
Game UX/UI
Designer
hero.sub: Seven years of building intuitive, immersive interfaces for games that millions of people play. From HUDs to onboarding flows, inventory systems to menus — every pixel has a purpose.

about.heading:
Designing for Players.
Thinking Like One.
paragraphs: I'm Monica Mae Dizon — a Game UX/UI Designer with 7+ years of experience crafting interfaces that disappear into the experience.

I specialize in designing for games: the unique challenge of building UI that serves real-time decision making, emotional pacing, and player progression — all without breaking immersion. I've worked across mobile, PC, and console platforms, collaborating with cross-functional teams of developers, artists, producers, and narrative designers to ship polished, player-centered experiences.

My process starts with understanding how players think, not just how they click. I use research, rapid iteration, and a deep obsession with game feel to build interfaces that are clear under pressure, satisfying to navigate, and aligned with the visual identity of each game's world.

I've contributed to 8 shipped titles across mobile RPGs, PC strategy, and console action-adventure. Whether embedded in a studio team or brought in as a specialist consultant, I show up ready to solve hard design problems — and make the game better for the people who play it.
about.skillsTitle: What I bring to your team

skills.desc: I design for players in motion, under stakes, mid-session

skills.title: High-fidelity UI craft

skills.title: Systems thinking
skills.desc: Scalable design languages that hold up across an entire game

skills.desc: Comfortable working closely with engineering, art, and production

skills.title: Research-informed iteration
skills.desc: Playtests, heuristics, and data shape every major decision

work.heading: The Work
work.intro: Each project below is a case study — not just screenshots, but the full story behind the design decisions, constraints, iterations, and outcomes. Explore screens, prototypes, and video walkthroughs that show what shipped and what it took to get there.

cases.title: Combat HUD Redesign
cases.meta: Project Nightfall · Yirisoft Studios · PC & Console — 2024
cases.challenge: Players were losing track of critical status effects mid-combat. The HUD needed a full information-hierarchy rebuild without adding visual noise.
designed: HUD layout and real-time feedback system
Status effect and cooldown visual language
Accessibility options for colorblind and low-vision players
cases.includes: Includes: wireframes · high-fidelity mockups · prototype videos · final in-game screenshots

cases.title: Inventory & Loadout System
cases.meta: Emberfall RPG · Indie Collab · Mobile & PC — 2023
cases.challenge: A deep crafting economy was buried in a four-level menu tree. Players needed to compare, equip, and craft in seconds — on a phone screen.
cases.role: Lead UX/UI Designer · Art Direction collaborator
cases.includes: Includes: wireframes · high-fidelity mockups · prototype videos · final in-game screenshots

cases.title: Onboarding & Tutorial Flow
cases.meta: Skyward Tactics · Mobile Studio · iOS & Android — 2022
cases.challenge: Day-1 retention was bleeding at the tutorial. The first-time user experience had to teach a complex tactics system without ever feeling like a lecture.
designed: Settings, accessibility options, and menu architecture
cases.includes: Includes: wireframes · high-fidelity mockups · prototype videos · final in-game screenshots

resume.heading:
Seven Years in the
Game Industry
resume.summary: Creative and efficient Game UX/UI Designer with 7 years of experience in the game industry. Proficient in transforming complex features into scalable, polished UI systems with smooth developer handoffs — and eager to bridge the gap between design and code.

experience.title: Game UX/UI Designer
bullets: Own the end-to-end UX/UI pipeline — high-fidelity game and web prototypes in Figma, from interactive flows to polished, user-centered experiences.
Established the product's foundational design systems with variables, color tokens, and shared component libraries.
Assist with UI implementation directly in Unreal Engine 5's UMG framework, bridging design and development.
Create and optimize motion graphics in Adobe After Effects with cross-browser compatibility.

experience.title: Mid 2D Artist
bullets: Led and mentored a cross-functional team of up to 11 artists across multiple concurrent game projects.
Designed UI assets — menus, buttons, HUD icons — and built interactive wireframes and prototypes to test usability before handoff.
Streamlined the team's design workflow with Figma components, auto-layout, and advanced plugins.

experience.title: Founder / Lead Artist
bullets: Created VTuber models and rigs using Clip Studio Paint and Live2D Cubism for startup clients.

bullets: 2D art, storyboards, UI assets, and game design mockups for clients including KidZania, NutriAsia, and ABS-CBN Studio Experience — plus a game developer internship building Unity proof-of-concepts.

contact.heading:
Let's Build Something
Players Will Love
contact.intro: Whether you're a studio looking for a design lead, a team that needs a specialist for a specific system, or a project in early development that needs a UX foundation — I'd love to hear about it.
contact.availabilityHighlight: open to full-time roles and freelance engagements
contact.availabilityAfter: and always happy to have a conversation before anything is formal.
contact.footnote: Response time: within 48 hours · NDA-friendly · Happy to share additional work samples upon request

footer.tagline: Designing the systems players feel — and never notice.
